title: Pricing & Reimbursement Sr Advisor
description:
Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor


**Category:** Market Access
**Location:**Roma, Lazio, IT  

Are you ready to shape how innovative therapies reach the patients who need them most in Italy? Join us as a Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor and play a pivotal role in securing market access for treatments that change lives across serious chronic diseases.

  


**Your new role**  

As Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor, you will be at the heart of our market access efforts in Italy, building strong relationships with key relevant stakeholders, while securing timely reimbursement for our portfolio. You will navigate the complexity of the Italian healthcare system and translate the value of our products into compelling submissions and negotiations with AIFA.



Day\-to\-day your tasks will include:


* Leading the end\-to\-end preparation, submission and negotiation of Pricing \& Reimbursement dossiers to AIFA, ensuring alignment with local regulations and corporate strategic goals
* Developing local pricing strategies and value propositions, including budget impact models, cost\-effectiveness analyses, HTA and HEOR tailored to the Italian healthcare system
* Building and maintaining relationships with key stakeholders to shape the external environment
* Partnering with Global and Regional teams to influence pricing assumptions and secure alignment on local launch strategies
* Communicating the unique attributes of our therapeutic area products to external multi\-stakeholders to defend and maximise the value proposition
* Collaborating cross\-functionally with Medical, Marketing, Regulatory and Market Access colleagues to integrate P\&R strategy across the product lifecycle
* Monitoring AIFA procedures, therapeutic registries, regional funding dynamics to anticipate risks and identify opportunities

  


**Your new department**  

As part of External Affairs Department, our commitment is to serve patients and build high\-value, sustainable partnerships across the healthcare ecosystem. As a highly integrated and strategic division, the External Affairs encompasses Pricing \& Reimbursement, Regional Market Access, Public Affairs, and External Communications. Given the dynamic and complex nature of our environment, cross\-functional collaboration is our cornerstone. Within External Affairs, you will join our affiliate in Rome, working closely with a dedicated team committed to ensuring that patients in Italy benefit from our innovative therapies as quickly as possible. You will contribute directly to shaping the access landscape and bringing transformative treatments to the Italian healthcare system.

  


**Your skills \& qualifications**  

To be successful in this position, you have a scientific background and a strong track record in Market Access in the Italian pharmaceutical environment. You combine technical expertise with a customer\-centric mindset and the ability to influence across stakeholders.


* A scientific background and a minimum of 5 years' experience in Market Access, HTA or HEOR with a clear focus on pricing and reimbursement
* Deep understanding of the Italian pharmaceutical regulatory framework, including AIFA procedures, therapeutic registries, regional funding dynamics
* Proven ability to design and execute integrated payer value propositions, pricing and HE analyses
* Customer\-centric mindset with excellent stakeholder management skills, both internally and with external key opinion leaders
* Highly performant with a strong focus on delivering great results, even in complex environments with few defined procedures
* Fluent in English and Italian, both written and spoken

  


**Working at Novo Nordisk**  

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long\-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

  


**What we offer**



At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.


**Salary:**For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 53,180\.00 to 82,430\.00 EUR, corresponding to the level of the position.



The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.


**Incentives and Benefits:**The salary package may include short\-term and/or long\-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.



Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

  


**Deadline**  

24 June 2026\. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible.


We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long\-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

title: WW Compensation & Benefits and HR Transformation Manager
description:
### **Position**

The WW Compensation \& Benefits and HR Transformation Manager will be responsible for shaping and governing a competitive and sustainable framework for compensation, benefits, workforce planning, and HR Tech systems, aligned with the company's positioning and business strategy.  



This role will involve active collaboration with Company Business Leaders and Group HR teams to ensure a fair and competitive reward structure, while establishing a robust workforce planning approach to anticipate staffing needs, monitor performance, and optimize workforce allocation. The role will also drive HR transformation initiatives by leveraging HRIS and AI to enhance data\-driven decision\-making and organizational effectiveness.


### **Job responsibilities**

Compensation, Benefits \& Reward Implementation:


* Deliver the worldwide Compensation, Benefits \& Reward strategy, ensuring its effective global implementation.
* Define, implement, and govern global compensation and benefits frameworks, ensuring alignment with Brand values, performance culture, and Group guidelines.
* Oversee short\-term and long\-term incentive schemes for corporate and retail populations, balancing pay\-for\-performance principles with luxury culture and the Group's sustainability strategy.

Compensation Management:


* Lead the job evaluation (job weighting) methodology and the annual salary review process, ensuring consistency with Group policies, internal equity, and alignment with market benchmarks across different geographies.
* Ensure internal equity, external competitiveness, and regulatory compliance across all geographies, with a strong focus on the EU pay transparency directive.

Workforce Planning \& Retail Staffing Governance:


* Design and implement a workforce planning framework with a strong focus on Retail, enabling proactive identification of staffing needs based on business performance, seasonality, and store\-specific drivers.
* Drive the evolution of staffing models through data analytics, ensuring optimal workforce allocation and cost efficiency while preserving service excellence.

HR Information Systems (HRIS), AI and Digital Transformation:


* Drive HR transformation initiatives by leveraging HRIS and AI technologies to enhance effectiveness, transparency, and predictive decision\-making.
* Lead the digitalization of HR processes, tools, and governance models in line with Group standards and best practices.
* Act as a key interface with Group stakeholders on reward policies, HR tech evolution, benchmarking, and governance topics.
### **Profile**

* Significant experience leading Compensation \& Benefits, Workforce Planning, and HRIS activities within complex, matrixed, and international organizations.
* Strong exposure to the Retail sector and/or consumer\-facing businesses; experience in luxury is a strong asset.
Proven track record in leading technological and HR transformation projects, including HRIS and AI\-driven initiatives, in international contexts.  
* 

Skills \& Competencies:


* Strategic and analytical mindset with strong business acumen.
* Ability to connect people, numbers, and operations.
* Credibility and discretion in matrix environments.
International mindset and cultural sensitivity.  
* 

Education \& Languages:


* Master’s degree in Economics, Finance, Business Administration, or equivalent.
Fluent English required; Italian highly recommended.  
* 

LVMH is committed to non\-discrimination and respect of each person’s singularities. We recognize and recruit all types of talent


***FENDI***
**MAISON**
----------------------

The Fendi Maison was established by Adele and Edoardo Fendi in Rome in 1925\. The opening of the first Fendi boutique – a handbag shop and fur workshop followed. Soon winning international acclaim, Fendi emerged as a brand renowned for its elegance, craftsmanship, innovation and style.
  

Called by the legendary five Fendi sisters, the collaboration with the late Karl Lagerfeld began back in 1965 and lasted 54 years. In 1992, Silvia Venturini Fendi seconded him in the Artistic Direction; in 1994, she is given the direction of Accessories and later of Menswear.
  

In 2000, the LVMH group acquires Fendi becoming in 2001 its majority shareholder. Appointed in 2020, Kim Jones held the role of Artistic Director of Couture and Womenswear until 2024\. Since 2020, Delfina Delettrez Fendi, fourth generation of the Fendi family, is Artistic Director of Jewellery. Today Fendi is synonymous with quality, tradition, experimentation and creativity.

description:
**Deadline for Applications**

GS6

**Standard Job Description**

Legal Associate
  

  

Organizational Setting and Work Relationships
  

The Legal Associate is a member of a protection team. S/he normally reports to a higher grade Legal Officer.
  

  

The Legal Associate assists the supervisor to prepare legal analysis required for quality, timely and effective protection responses to the needs of populations of concern. Another important function of the position is to contribute to the involvement of the persons of concern with an office in making decisions that affect them, whether in accessing their rights or in identifying appropriate solutions to their problems. To achieve this, the incumbent will need to support involvement of communities of concern and relevant authorities in continuous cooperation. Additionally, the Legal Associate is expected to contribute to the capacity building activities for local and central state authorities in the area of protection of persons of concern either directly or in cooperation with partners.
  

  

The Legal Associate assists an office in providing quality, timely and effective protection responses to the needs of populations of concern. S/he is also required to assist an office in ensuring that persons of concern are involved with the office in making decisions that affect them, whether in accessing their rights or in identifying appropriate solutions to their problems. In addition, the Legal Associate is in charge of the office's corporate legal work and advises on all matters of Labour Law affecting refugees.
  

  

All UNHCR staff members are accountable to perform their duties as reflected in their job description. They do so within their delegated authorities, in line with the regulatory framework of UNHCR which includes the UN Charter, UN Staff Regulations and Rules, UNHCR Policies and Administrative Instructions as well as relevant accountability frameworks. In addition, staff members are required to discharge their responsibilities in a manner consistent with the core, functional, cross\-functional and managerial competencies and UNHCR's core values of professionalism, integrity and respect for diversity.
  

  

Duties
  

* Monitor the development of law and doctrine.
* Provide input to assist in analysis of relevant emerging legislation in line with protection and humanitarian principles, international law and standards.
* Provide inputs to selected advocacy efforts of the operation.
* Contribute to the preparation of legal advice and draft position papers.
* Consistently apply international and national law and UNHCR policies and standards.
* Liaise with relevant state authorities at central and local levels, partner NGOs and other relevant stakeholders.
* Participate in meetings within UNHCR and with external counterparts at the relevant level.
* Contribute to draft protection documents and policies in cooperation with supervisor.
* Provide guidance and support in the formulation of protection strategies.
* Assist in handling individual cases and situations raising special questions of application or interpretation of international law, refugee and human rights law.
* Prepare protection documents and policies in cooperation with supervisor.
* Participate in the development of project proposals and project implementation.
* Assist in the preparation of legal advice and drafting of position papers and guidelines on pertinent legal and operational issues.
* Contribute to advocacy towards proper interpretation of law and doctrine as well as in the development of standards of protection.
* Consistently apply international and national law and UNHCR policies and standards.
* Liaise and intervene with local authorities on the legal issues pertaining to persons of concern.
* Assist in providing legal advice on all cases referred from all units of UNHCR, most notably RSD, RST and Registration.
* Support the identification and management of risks and seek to seize opportunities impacting objectives in the area of responsibility. Ensure decision making in risk based in the functional area of work. Raise risks, issues and concerns to a supervisor or to relevant functional colleague(s).
* Perform other related duties as required.

  

Minimum Qualifications
  

  

Years of Experience / Degree Level
  

For G6 \- 3 years relevant experience with High School Diploma; or 2 years relevant work experience with Bachelor or equivalent or higher
  

  

Field(s) of Education
  

Not applicable.
  

  

Certificates and/or Licenses
  

Law, Human Rights, Political Science
  

(Certificates and Licenses marked with an asterisk\* are essential)
  

  

Relevant Job Experience
  

Essential
  

Previous relevant legal related work experience.
  

Desirable
  

Experience in facilitating training.
  

Completion of UNHCR learning programmes on protection or any other equivalent external courses.
  

  

Functional Skills
  

* CO\-Drafting and Documentation
* LE\-Legal Research

TR\-Training \- Virtual and face to face
  

(Functional Skills marked with an asterisk\* are essential)
  

  

Language Requirements
  

For International Professional and Field Service jobs: Knowledge of English and UN working language of the duty station if not English.
  

For National Professional jobs: Knowledge of English and UN working language of the duty station if not English and local language.
  

For General Service jobs: Knowledge of English and/or UN working language of the duty station if not English.  

  

  

  

All UNHCR workforce members must individually and collectively, contribute towards a working environment where each person feels safe, and empowered to perform their duties. This includes by demonstrating no tolerance for sexual exploitation and abuse, harassment including sexual harassment, sexism, gender inequality, discrimination and abuse of power.
  

  

As individuals and as managers, all must be proactive in preventing and responding to inappropriate conduct, support ongoing dialogue on these matters and speaking up and seeking guidance and support from relevant UNHCR resources when these issues arise.
  

  

This is a Standard Job Description for all UNHCR jobs with this job title and grade level. The Operational Context may contain additional essential and/or desirable qualifications relating to the specific operation and/or position. Any such requirements are incorporated by reference in this Job Description and will be considered for the screening, shortlisting and selection of candidates.
**Desired Candidate Profile**

OPERATIONAL CONTEXT
  

  

UNHCR’s Office in Italy is a multi\-Country office responsible for Italy, San Marino, the Holy See, Cyprus and Malta. Italy plays an important role in refugee protection in Europe, remaining one of the major points of arrival for persons seeking protection in Europe, mostly arriving by sea, via the Mediterranean.
  

  

In 2024, 66\.617 migrants and refugees originating from 57 countries arrived in Italy by sea and Italy received almost 50% of all sea arrivals to Southern Europe through the Mediterranean Sea. In 2025, sea arrivals to Italy were recorded at 66\.316, remaining stable compared to 2024\. The primary countries of origin for these arrivals included Bangladesh, Egypt and Eritrea.
  

  

The number of asylum applications lodged in Italy rose, in 2024, to the record number of nearly 159\.000, dropping to 138\.000 in 2025\. The RSD system, from the access phase to second instance, is facing historical challenges in ensuring fairness and efficiency of the procedure, while preserving its integrity and safeguarding timely access to status recognition and inclusion for people in need of international protection. The EU Pact on Migration and Asylum will become applicable as of 12 June 2026\.
  

  

In this context, the incumbent, under the supervision of the Senior Protection Associate (RSD and statelessness), will contribute to MCO Italy’s engagement with national authorities in view of a protection\-sensitive implementation of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum. In particular, s/he will coordinate the project, jointly implemented by UNHCR and IOM, aimed at providing technical\-legal support, through the deployment of legal researchers, to Specialized Divisions on Immigration and international protection, in second instance RSD, detention validation and any other relevant judicial procedure, in line with the novelties introduced by the Pact. The incumbent will oversee the coordination of UNHCR’s legal researchers, as well as the delivery of training and other capacity development and technical support activities, based on relevant international and EU law standards and in line with UNHCR’s relevant guidance and positions. S/he will closely coordinate with IOM project manager, thus ensuring joint planning, strategic implementation of the activities and communication with the Ministry of Justice, continuous monitoring of the quality of the support provided at local level, and the adoption of a harmonized approach across all resources (UNHCR and IOM). At the same time, the incumbent will contribute to ensuring capacity development, consistency and high\-quality standards of the whole system, notably by promoting regular communication and robust networking amongst all legal researchers (including IOM’s and EUAA’s), sharing of good practices and relevant materials.  

  

  

  

DESIRED CANDIDATE PROFILE
  

  

MCO Italy is looking for an extremely solid candidate with excellent legal expertise in international and EU refugee law and, notably, thorough knowledge of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum. The candidate should have proven understanding of the Italian operational context as well as expertise of the asylum system in the country. S/he must be proficient in Italian and English, have excellent legal research, legal drafting and reporting skills in both languages, and have a creative, solution and result\-oriented outlook to successfully perform the role and coordinate with national authorities, other UN and EU agencies, and relevant UNHCR colleagues. The candidate should have the ability to think and execute strategically and possess strong institutional and interpersonal communication skills. RSD and other protection experiences would be an asset.  

  

  

  

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION
  

  

Candidates must be legally authorized to work in Italy.
  

English is the official working language of UNHCR in Italy. Only applications fully completed in English will be considered.
  

Only short\-listed candidates will be contacted.
  

Assessment of short\-listed candidates might comprise of both an interview and a written test.
  

UNHCR is committed to diversity and welcomes applications from qualified candidates regardless of disability, gender identity, marital or civil partnership status, race, colour or ethnic and national origins, religion or belief, or sexual orientation.
  

We welcome applications from candidates with a refugee or stateless background.
  

UNHCR has a ZERO TOLERANCE policy on sexual exploitation and abuse, and sexual harassment. Successful candidates will be subject to mandatory UN Sexual Exploitation and Abuse and Sexual Harassment clearance check prior to receiving an offer.
  

UNHCR does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process (application, interview meeting, processing, training or any other fees).
  

All applications will be treated with the strictest confidentiality.

 VN8455

**Purpose**


As a Sales Support Associate, you will play a critical role in supporting European sales efforts for both direct and indirect markets. Your position is essential in ensuring the smooth operation and success of our sales team by providing comprehensive administrative and operational support. You will be responsible for assisting with sales activities, managing customer inquiries, processing orders, and maintaining accurate records. By coordinating with various departments, you will help to streamline sales processes and enhance customer satisfaction. Your efforts will contribute to the overall achievement of sales targets and the growth of our business in the European market.  

  

Offered Salary Range: €28\.000 – 32\.000  

CCNL: Chimico Farmaceutico

 **Key Responsibilities**


PRICE ACTIVITIES


* Support Sales management strategy for price management, entering price offers on the Company's ERP (agreement or price list) utilising processes developed as instructed by the team coordinator.
* Review price offers received from sales reps or sales managers, to ensure they are compliant with our internal policies and ensure they are saved and uploaded inside the system as indicated by the coordinator.


TENDERS (Administration of International tenders)


* Through proactive review of Tender Managements system (country relevant) apply for appropriate tenders on a daily basis.
* Co\-ordinate with Sales to structure price offer.
* Review, add to and organise all supporting documents required for completion of tenders (liaising with Sales, Marketing, QA, Regulatory etc.)
* Timely manage the samples requirement
* Proactive follow up on contracts ensuring price updates are effected in timely manner
* Ensure Sales team(s) appraised of outcomes, develop and maintain proactive notification for contract renewals
* For direct markets create turnover declarations for purchasing groups and tender businesses, submitting to finance for audit approval.


ADMIN TASKS


* Deal with various local admin tasks based on demand
* Any customer mailings – translations, mail merge
* Organise shipments of capital equipment demos
* Complete tasks given by line manager within agreed timescales
* Interpret \& respond clearly, effectively \& professionally to spoken requests by phone or in person, and to verbal or written instructions
* Perform duties in an efficient, professional and courteous manner
* Maintain regular consistent attendance, punctuality, professional appearance, and adherence to relevant health \& safety procedures
  

  

**Qualification**

* Fluent in Italian and English
* Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field is preferred, but not mandatory
* Proven experience in a sales support or administrative role, preferably within a sales or customer service environment
* Attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and record\-keeping
* Familiarity with sales processes and the ability to support sales teams effectively
* Knowledge of order processing, inventory management, and logistics
  

  

**Company**


BVI® is refocusing the future of vision.  

  

As one of the fastest\-growing, diversified surgical ophthalmic businesses in the world, our purpose\-built portfolio of trusted brands includes: Beaver® (Knives and Blades), Visitec® (Cannulas), Malosa® (Single\-Use Instruments), Vitreq® (Vitreoretinal Surgical Products) and PhysIOL® (Premium IOLs), and spans more than 90 countries.  

  

We’ve set our sights on touching the lives of millions of patients affected by conditions such as cataracts, refractive error, glaucoma, retinal disease, and dry eye. Unburdened by legacy or bureaucracy, we have developed our strategy around a simple concept – taking pride in delivering innovative solutions for our physicians and patients, based on their needs. We trust and empower our associates to make decisions and solve problems because collaboration drives us. Valuing agility, simplicity, and transparency, we stay committed to listening to our customers, delivering for our patients, and keeping the future in focus.  

  

Learn more at www.bvimedical.com

title: Sr. Spclst, Value Market Access New Products Oncology
description:
Job Description

**Senior Specialist, Value Access New Product Oncology**


Il/La Senior Specialist, Value Access New Product Oncology contribuisce, in linea con la strategia aziendale e sotto la supervisione del Value Access Director, alla preparazione, pianificazione ed implementazione delle attività di accesso per i prodotti dell’area terapeutica New Product Oncology. Sviluppa e adatta , in sinergia e contatto con global, contenuti farmacoeconomici, di Health Technology Assessment e di outcome research, supportando la definizione della proposizione di valore del prodotto e la predisposizione dei documenti, dei dossier e degli strumenti necessari a favorire l’accesso dei farmaci a livello nazionale, regionale e locale.

**Principali aree di responsabilità:**


In linea con gli obiettivi aziendali, il/la Senior Specialist, Value Access New Product Oncology:

* Collabora con i team cross\-funzionali nella definizione della strategia di accesso e nella costruzione dei messaggi di valore dei prodotti dell’area terapeutica di competenza, portando il punto di vista dei payer e dei decision maker rilevanti per il contesto italiano.
* Analizza a livello nazionale e regionale i fattori che ostacolano o favoriscono la corretta percezione del valore dei farmaci della sua area, identificando opportunità e criticità di accesso e contribuendo alla definizione dei relativi piani di azione.
* Contribuisce, nelle fasi pre\-lancio e lancio, alla raccolta, generazione e adattamento delle evidenze epidemiologiche, economiche e organizzative necessarie a supportare la value proposition del prodotto.
* Sviluppa e adatta, con crescente autonomia, modelli farmacoeconomici e di valutazione del valore (ad esempio Budget Impact Model, Cost\-Effectiveness Analysis e Cost\-Utility Analysis) al contesto nazionale, in coordinamento con le funzioni globali, regionali e locali.
* Supporta la definizione di piani di evidence generation e dissemination, inclusi studi osservazionali, analisi epidemiologiche, valutazioni di impatto economico e altri progetti utili a documentare il valore dei prodotti e a colmare eventuali data gap.
* Collabora alla predisposizione di dossier, materiali e strumenti a supporto dei processi di Pricing \& Reimbursement e delle attività di Health Technology Assessment, assicurandone coerenza con la strategia di accesso e con i requisiti del contesto italiano.
* Contribuisce allo sviluppo di proposte progettuali e di interventi formativi rivolti agli stakeholder del sistema sanitario, in coerenza con i bisogni identificati e nel rispetto delle procedure aziendali, di compliance e legali.
* Supporta il monitoraggio del contesto regolatorio, HTA e di accesso a livello nazionale e regionale, contribuendo a identificare trend, rischi e opportunità rilevanti per i prodotti di competenza.
* Collabora con le funzioni locali e globali nella gestione delle attività connesse ai processi di EU\-HTA e Joint Clinical Assessment, per quanto di competenza del ruolo.
* Contribuisce alla costruzione di relazioni professionali efficaci con i principali interlocutori istituzionali e tecnico\-scientifici coinvolti nei processi di HTA e accesso, a livello nazionale, regionale e locale.

 **Requisiti minimi**

* Laurea in discipline economiche e/o scientifiche.
* Formazione e background in economia sanitaria, farmacoeconomia, pricing \& reimbursement e Health Technology Assessment. Costituisce un plus un Master in economia sanitaria, farmacoeconomia o HTA.
* Almeno 3 anni di esperienza in ruoli di Market Access, preferibilmente in ambito farmaceutico e con esposizione all’area oncologica; saranno considerate positivamente anche esperienze rilevanti maturate in consulenza o in contesti accademici.
* Conoscenza dei principali strumenti e approcci di valutazione farmacoeconomica e di outcome research.
* Buona capacità organizzativa e di project management, con attitudine a gestire attività multiple e priorità in contesti cross\-funzionali.
* Capacità analitiche e di sintesi, unite a un approccio operativo e orientato al risultato.
* Buone capacità comunicative, relazionali e di negoziazione, con interlocutori interni ed esterni differenti.
* Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese.
* Ottima conoscenza di Excel e PowerPoint.
* Capacità di lavorare in team, proattività e orientamento all’apprendimento continuo.

title: Quality Assurance Associate
description:
**Lo.Li. Pharma International**, azienda leader nel settore nutraceutico con una forte presenza internazionale, è alla ricerca di un/una Quality Assurance Associate.

  

  

**Descrizione generale**  

Sotto la supervisione del Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio, la risorsa fornirà supporto locale per le attività di Quality Assurance, garantendo la conformità agli standard interni ed esterni. La risorsa selezionata collaborerà alle attività tecniche legate alla Qualità sull'intero portafoglio prodotti di Lo.Li. Pharma International, supportando le diverse fasi del ciclo di vita del prodotto.

  

**Principali responsabilità**  

In accordo con il Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio provvede ad effettuare le seguenti attività:
* Mantiene e migliora i processi di qualità esistenti per garantire un funzionamento sempre adeguato/completo dei processi di qualità.
* Lavora a stretto contatto con gli altri reparti per garantire la conformità agli attuali sistemi di change control, CAPA, non conformità, reclami, follow\-up delle osservazioni degli audit interni.
* Svolge indagini su non conformità e reclami sulla qualità dei prodotti.
* Contribuisce allo sviluppo di piani di azioni correttive e preventive e monitora l’attuazione delle stesse entro un determinato lasso di tempo.
* Aggiorna, mantiene e migliora i documenti di processo e la documentazione del sistema di gestione della qualità in conformità ai requisiti ISO e GMP.
* Esegue analisi dei dati, prepara trend di qualità e riferisce i risultati alle parti interessate.
* Documenta le attività di assicurazione qualità garantendo l’adempimento agli standard qualitativi aziendali mediante l’esecuzione di audit interni.
* Segue gli studi di stabilità dei prodotti in accordo alle indicazioni e ai protocolli definiti con il Responsabile;
* Definisce e revisiona gli aspetti qualitativi dei prodotti con i clienti e con i produttori e valuta la conformità degli stessi nei prodotti di riferimento;
* Definisce e revisiona i Quality Agreement e le Specifiche tecniche dei prodotti nei contratti con i clienti;
* Collabora con i siti produttivi e con le figure responsabili della Lo.Li. Pharma Italia per l’ottenimento della documentazione attinente alla qualità dei prodotti (schede tecniche prodotto finito, draft CoA, ST materie prime, materiale di packaging ecc) e si occupa del corretto salvataggio di tutta la documentazione in accordo con le indicazioni ricevute dal Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio;
* Riceve e verifica tutta la documentazione di qualità necessaria per il rilascio dei prodotti sul mercato (CoA, CoC, contro\-campioni, batch release, batch record ecc), condividendola con i colleghi interessati;
* Verifica e gestisce le segnalazioni ricevute dai clienti e/o assegnatoli dal Responsabile, interfacciandosi con i clienti e i produttori; prepara i report di non\-conformità/deviazione/reclamo/change control da condividere con il cliente dopo approvazione del Responsabile e salva tutta la documentazione relativa in accordo con le indicazioni del Responsabile;
* Supporta il Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio nella stesura delle procedure relative alla propria area di competenza o delle altre aree aziendali garantendo aderenza ai principi della ISO 9001 e ai requisiti GMP.
* Supporta le aree interne ed i clienti internazionali negli aspetti di Qualità.
* Revisiona e presenta mediante report mensili al Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio lo stato di avanzamento delle attività di Assicurazione Qualità.
* Ulteriori compiti di volta in volta dovuti a esigenze aziendali o di reparto su input del Responsabile Qualità e Regolatorio.


  

**Requisiti**
* Laurea in discipline scientifiche
* Ottimo livello di inglese (scritto e parlato)
* Costituisce titolo preferenziale una precedente esperienza in Quality Assurance nel settore farmaceutico/nutraceutico
* Capacità di lavorare efficacemente in un contesto operativo dinamico
* Spiccate doti relazionali e attitudine alla collaborazione
* Ottime capacità organizzative
* Efficaci capacità di comunicazione scritta e verbale


  

**Luogo di lavoro**: Roma, Via Tiburtina n. 912  

  

**Tipo contratto**: a tempo indeterminato, 2° livello CCNL Terziario Distribuzione e Servizi (Confcommercio), RAL 30\.000–34\.000 in funzione dell'esperienza, buoni pasto da 8 euro.  

  

**Cosa offriamo**
* Un ambiente di lavoro dinamico e internazionale
* Opportunità di crescita e formazione continua
* Un team coeso e orientato alla qualità

  

  

Se sei una persona motivata, attenta ai dettagli e appassionata della qualità, inviaci la tua candidatura!

title: T3 Project Lead
description:
**Job title: T3 Project Lead**

**Location**: Anagni, Italy

**About the Job**


As T3 Project Lead within our Engineering \& Maintenance Team, you’ll be part of a team of qualified professionals, managing strategic engineering projects and ensuring operational excellence of the facilities.


Ready to get started?

  



**About Sanofi:**


We’re an R\&D\-driven, AI\-powered biopharma company committed to improving people’s lives and delivering compelling growth. Our deep understanding of the immune system – and innovative pipeline – enables us to invent medicines and vaccines that treat and protect millions of people around the world. Together, we chase the miracles of science to improve people’s lives.

**Main Responsibilities:**

* Lead Tier 3 capital project studies including driving each stage gate review, supporting site, and M\&S decision making process
* End\-to\-end capital project delivery for projects up to €5M including initiation, feasibility, design, execution, C\&Q, handover, \& close\-out
* To develop Block Flow Diagram, Process Flow Diagram, material and energy balances, P\&ID’s, data sheets of equipment, of instruments and of safety devices, Technical Specification/URS for Request for Quotation, Technical alignment of vendors/supplier’s quotation.
* Monthly capital project reporting on all project KPIs for safety, cost, schedule, and resources
* Adherence to all mandatory Sanofi and local \& regulatory compliance standards and policies, including construction safety GMRs, Planet Care standards, GMP, and FDA regulations/standards
* Foster a safety first, quality always culture throughout the project implementing SMS 2\.0 and TRT ways of working
* Ensure staffing of all engineering project resources and align cross\-function strategic workforce plan to delivery projects

**About You**

* **Experience**: capital project engineering and/or project delivery experience as a Technical Engineer in pharmaceutical manufacturing, process engineering, and/or building \& utilities.
* **Soft and technical skills**: Project management experience managing process \& construction safety, budgets, schedules, risk, and resources for multip discpliine capex project; regulatory knowledge with familiarity of GMP, FDA, ISPE guidelines, building \& construction regulations/standards; stakeholder \& People Management skills working cross\-functionally in a matrix organization (Maintenance, Priduction, Validation, Validation assurance, Utilties, Planning etc.); project Management/Project Controls skills managing budgets, schedules; commissioning and Qualification knowledge; black utilities knowledge; HVAC knowledge; pharma processes knowledge; automation knowledge; Electrical knowledge; computer literate (ACAD, Microsoft project, word, excel, PowerPoint); communication skills, changes evaluation abilities, risks analysis abilities, and resources planning.
* **Education**: Chemical, Mechanical, or Electrical engineering degree with a professional engineering designation preferred, e.g., PE, P.Eng. Charter Eng.
* **Languages**: Fluency in Italian and English (additional languages are a plus)

***Why choose us?***

* Be part of a pioneering biopharma company where patient insights shape drug development.
* Work at the forefront of AI\-powered science that accelerates discovery and improves outcomes.
* Collaborate beyond your expertise, sparking new ideas with diverse, multidisciplinary teams.
* Explore bold career paths – with opportunities to grow through scientific, leadership, and cross\-functional moves.
* Care for yourself and your loved ones with generous health and well\-being benefits, including top\-tier healthcare and 14 weeks’ gender\-neutral parental leave.

**Pursue** *Progress*. **Discover** *Extraordinary*.


Join Sanofi and step into a new era of science \- where your growth can be just as transformative as the work we do. We invest in you to reach further, think faster, and do what’s never\-been\-done\-before. You’ll help push boundaries, challenge convention, and build smarter solutions that reach the communities we serve. Ready to chase the miracles of science and improve people’s lives? Let’s Pursue *Progress* and Discover *Extraordinary* – together.


At Sanofi, we provide equal opportunities to all regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.


\#LI\-EUR


\#LI\-Onsite
company_description: We chase the miracles of science every single day, pursuing progress to make a real impact on millions of patients around the world.

title: Visiting Associate (fixed term contract), Italy
description:
Who We Are
  
  

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963\. Today, we help clients with total transformation\-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom\-line impact.
  
  

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading\-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.
  
  

\#LI\-DNI
  
  

What You'll Do
  
  

Join us for 6 months as a Visiting Associate!
  
  

You will be a fundamental part of the team and work alongside some of the world’s top minds in the industry, significantly contributing to the development of diverse topics and support our ambitious growth targets. In this context, you will work hand in hand with C\-levels and key decision makers of our clients, while playing a critical role in the firm's project teams, contributing to the analysis, design, and implementation of business strategies for clients across various industries.
  
  

**You will:**  

Work on impactful projects that combine a deep understanding of the business with BCG’s trademark strategic expertise
  
Be part of a team partnering with leading clients to provide solutions to business\-critical challenges
  
Contribute to the day\-to\-day execution of consulting projects, ensuring the successful delivery of client requirements within the scope of the engagement
  
Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis for the project execution
  
Prepare and present findings, insights, and recommendations to both the BCG project team and client stakeholders.
  
Work across a range of industries and functions
  
  

What You'll Bring
  
  

We’re looking for exceptional talent to join us. You would typically have:
  
  

Graduated with a Master of Science or Master's degree
  
At least 6 months work experience in structured context
  
Strong academic track record
  
Strong logical and analytical skills with outstanding number mindset, with a proactive and creative attitude
  
Effective in working in a team
  
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in Italian and English
  
Advanced knowledge of MS Office suite (Excel and PowerPoint at excellent level)
  
Familiarity with GenAI tools
  
  

Additional info
  
  

Additional info
  
  

At BCG, our people and relationships are at the heart of everything we do. We believe that in\-person collaboration plays an essential role in our culture, mentorship, and professional development. That's why we generally operate on a hybrid model, with office presence shaped by business needs, team norms, and local policies and practices. We aim to provide a dynamic and collaborative environment that fosters connection and teamwork.
  
  

Compensation Information
  
  

Total compensation for this role includes base salary and a benefits package described below. Our Reward Philosophy is to take a holistic approach to offer a comprehensive package which includes market\-competitive base salary and benefits that can support both physical and mental health. Our philosophy drives our actions and aims at being competitive, fair and easy for employees to understand. We are committed to ensuring that BCG is the best place for you to thrive and grow.
  
  

The annual base salary for this role is EUR 29\.800 (pro\-rated based on the duration of the employment contract).
  
  

BCG offers a best\-in\-class benefits package designed to provide comprehensive care and meaningful support to you and your family. Benefits may vary based on role, contract type, and eligibility criteria.
  
  

**Our benefits include:**  

**Health insurance:** comprehensive medical coverage for employees with the option to extend coverage to eligible family members.
  
**Meal vouchers and corporate discounts:** daily meal allowance and access to a range of partner discounts.
  
Life / Accident / Disability Insurance for employees
  
**Employee Assistance Program (EAP):** confidential psychological, legal, and financial support services.
  
**Well\-being initiatives:** programs supporting physical health, mental well\-being, and family needs.
  
  

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
company_description: Boston Consulting Group (BCG) is a global management consulting firm with 100+ offices around the world. Our diverse, global teams are passionate about unlocking potential and making change happen.

title: Esperto Diritto Tributario
description:
Per favorire la crescita professionale, Alma Laboris Business School propone il **Percorso per** E**sperti in Diritto e Tecniche Tributarie (in Aula oppure Online \- Live Streaming)** per un approccio Executive a Fiscalità, Tributi, Accertamento e Contenzioso con l’obiettivo di:


* Fornire **Conoscenze Tecniche**, attraverso l’esame di numerosi casi aziendali, esercitazioni pratiche e la presenza di Esperti del settore;
* Costruire **Opportunità di Carriera,** con il supporto gratuito di Consulenti qualificati;


**Le principali Materie trattate nel Percorso in formula week\-end:**


* La Tassazione delle Persone Fisiche e delle Società di Persone
* Imposte Indirette e Fiscalita’ dei Patrimoni e dei Trust
* Il Bilancio di Esercizio ed il Sistema di Tassazione delle Società di Capitali
* Fiscalità Straordinaria di Impresa
* Fiscalità dei Gruppi Societari
* Attività Ispettiva, di Controllo e di Accertamento del Tributo
* Il Processo Tributario
* La Riscossione Tributaria e le Sanzioni Amministrative Tributarie
* Istituti Preventivi e Deflattivi del Contenzioso Tributario
* Diritto Tributario Comunitario
* Iva Nazionale
* Scambi Internazionali, Iva e Dogane
* Diritto Tributario Internazionale
* Diritto Penale Tributario e Legislazione Antiriciclaggio


**Opportunità di Carriera:**



Alma Laboris, già durante il Percorso, propone per ogni singolo Partecipante:


* **Promozione del Profilo alle Aziende** interessate a fissare colloqui per diverse posizioni aperte;
* **Consulenza di Carriera qualificata**, per delineare con efficacia l’ambito di spendibilità;
* Portale “Network”, strumento esclusivo ideato per proporre in autonomia la candidatura agli Annunci di lavoro che le Aziende Partner e la Business School pubblicano periodicamente;
* **Assistenza individuale** con un Tutor esperto delle dinamiche del mercato, per consigli utili e strumenti aggiornati;


**Con la candidatura all'annuncio riceveranno informazioni sulle attività formative.**



Il presente annuncio è rivolto a entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03\.



(Privacy: accetto che le informazioni inviate siano gestite da Alma Laboris Business School in ottemperanza al GDPR \- General Data Protection Regulation \- www.almalaboris.com/privacy.html).

title: Presales Engineer | Aerospace
description:
Job description
  
  

Vuoi iniziare una nuova avventura professionale come Pre\-Sales Engineer – Defence \& Telco Technologies in una delle nostre opportunità nel settore Telecommunications \& Defense a Roma?
  
  

Cosa farai?
  
* Gestire attività di pre\-sales per soluzioni tecnologiche in ambito difesa, con focus su tecnologie delle telecomunicazioni (es. impianti di videosorveglianza, anti\-intrusione, radar, antenne).
* Analizzare tender e documentazione di gara, valutando la fattibilità tecnica ed economica della partecipazione.
* Identificare e confrontare fornitori e componenti hardware (es. radar, antenne, apparati telco) per costruire la migliore soluzione tecnico\-economica.
* Collaborare con il team Sales nella definizione della strategia di offerta e nella preparazione della documentazione di gara.
* Redigere l’offerta tecnica e contribuire alla stesura della proposta complessiva per l’aggiudicazione delle gare.
* Interfacciarti con stakeholder interni (sales, tecnico, procurement) per allineare requisiti cliente, vincoli tecnici e budget.
* Supportare presentazioni tecniche verso il cliente in fase di pre\-vendita, quando richiesto.
* Contribuire al miglioramento continuo dei processi e delle best practice pre\-sales in ambito difesa/telco.


✍️ Cosa richiediamo?
  
* Laurea in Ingegneria delle Telecomunicazioni, Elettronica o discipline affini, o esperienza equivalente.
* Almeno 3 anni di esperienza come Pre\-Sales Engineer nel settore difesa, preferibilmente su tecnologie telco (videosorveglianza, anti\-intrusione, radar, antenne, reti di comunicazione).
* Solida conoscenza del contesto telco e delle principali tecnologie di telecomunicazione applicate alla difesa.
* Esperienza concreta nella gestione di tender/gare: analisi requisiti, valutazione fattibilità, definizione offerta tecnica.
* Conoscenza della lingua italiana fluente e inglese almeno a livello B1/B2\.


**Nice to have:**  

* Esperienza di interfaccia con clienti istituzionali o grandi player del settore difesa.
* Familiarità con processi di procurement pubblico e normative di settore.
* Buone capacità di comunicazione, negoziazione e lavoro in team cross\-funzionali.


✨ Che cosa offriamo?
  
* Una comunità internazionale che riunisce oltre 110 nazionalità diverse.
* Un ambiente in cui la fiducia è centrale e la crescita interna è valorizzata.
* Un solido sistema di formazione con Academy interna e oltre 250 moduli disponibili.
* Un ambiente di lavoro vivace con eventi interni (afterwork, team building, ecc.).
* Un forte impegno nella CSR, in particolare attraverso la partecipazione al nostro programma WeCare Together.


**Benefici locali in Italia:**  

* Buoni pasto.
* Programma di welfare con sconti e vantaggi per le persone dipendenti.
* Opzioni di lavoro flessibili secondo policy aziendale (politica di lavoro ibrido, ove compatibile con il progetto).
* Assicurazione sanitaria e programmi di benessere, ove applicabili.


Remunerazione e trasparenza
  
* Gross Annual Salary Range / RAL iniziale fissa: EUR 24\.000 – EUR 36\.000\.
* CCNL del Commercio.
* La fascia retributiva è definita secondo criteri oggettivi e neutri rispetto al genere.
* Componente variabile: eventuali premi discrezionali non garantiti non inclusi nella RAL indicata.
* Durante il processo di selezione non chiederemo informazioni sulla tua retribuzione attuale o precedente.


Le nostre fasce retributive sono volutamente ampie, perché cerchiamo persone di talento, non “profili standard”. Uno stesso ruolo può attrarre candidature molto diverse tra loro, e crediamo sia importante lasciare spazio a questa varietà. La collocazione all’interno della fascia dipenderà dalla tua esperienza specifica, dalle tue competenze e da ciò che potrai portare nel ruolo.
  
  

**La persona ideale si riconosce in questi valori:**  

**Boldness:** il nostro spirito di avventura imprenditoriale è ciò che ci unisce tutti e ci porta al successo.
  
**Trust:** la fiducia è il fondamento di tutte le relazioni, la fiducia ci consente di promuovere la libertà di espressione, creatività e innovazione.
  
**Performance:** questo si traduce in un desiderio costante di andare avanti e non smettere mai di chiedere di più a noi stessi.
  
**Commitment:** non ci limitiamo a rispettare i nostri impegni e lavoriamo duramente per ottenere grandi risultati.
  
**Independence:** Amaris Consulting appartiene a chi l'ha creata e a chi la sviluppa ogni giorno.
  
  

Candidandoti per questa posizione, potresti avere l’opportunità di entrare a far parte di un’azienda che supporta i propri partner nei settori Defence, Telco, Automotive \& Motorsport, Avionic \& Aerospace, Railway, Industrial, Domotica e Robotica, con attività di ingegneria e consulenza nelle aree Development \& Design, Testing \& Validation, System Engineering, R\&D e Project Management.
  
  

Diversità e Inclusione
  
Amaris Consulting promuove pari opportunità e valuta le candidature sulla base di competenze, esperienza e potenziale. Accogliamo candidature qualificate senza discriminazioni legate a genere, identità o espressione di genere, orientamento sessuale, origine etnica, nazionalità, religione o convinzioni personali, età, disabilità, stato civile o familiare, o altre caratteristiche protette.
  
  

Se hai bisogno di adattamenti ragionevoli durante il processo di selezione, comunicacelo: faremo il possibile per supportarti.
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting è una società di consulenza tecnologica indipendente che accompagna e fornisce soluzioni alle imprese.
  
Riunisce più di 7\.500 persone distribuite in 5 continenti e oltre 60 paesi. Con oltre 1\.000 clienti in tutto il mondo, implementiamo soluzioni per grandi progetti da oltre un decennio.
  
I nostri esperti coprono tutti i settori, dai servizi finanziari ai trasporti, dalla sanità alla tecnologia.
  
Amaris è la “stepping stone” che consente di attraversare il guado, il trampolino che permette di proiettarti verso i tuoi obiettivi, affrontare le sfide e realizzare con successo tutti i tuoi progetti.
company_description: Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. It gathers more than 7,500 people across 5 continents and more than 60 countries.

title: Meccanico/a Automotive, Roma
description:
**What to Expect**
Stiamo cercando Meccanici/che Automotive di talento per i Service Center Tesla in Italia. Il team Tecnico Tesla é la spina dorsale delle operazioni di assistenza, a supporto della nostra missione di accelerare la transizione ad un mondo di fonti di energia sostenibili. Abbiamo creato la flotta di veicoli piú innovativa di sempre e tu, nel ruolo di Tecnico/a Service, ci aiuterai a garantire un servizio parimenti innovativo alla nostra clientela.


Cosa offriamo:

* Un ambiente di lavoro sicuro e dinamico, in cui collaborazione, inclusione ed apprendimento sono aspetti chiave per avere successo
* La possibilitá di lavorare con tecnologie all'avanguardia e strumenti e software avanzati
* Formazione continua e piani di sviluppo, per aiutarti a migliorare le tue competenze e la tua carriera
* Benefits interessanti ed un pacchetto retributivo competitivo, che include azioni Tesla
  

**What You'll Do*** Occuparti della manutenzione, riparare e migliorare i veicoli: lavorando con software specifico Tesla e strumenti avanzati, eseguirai riparazioni ed interventi di manutenzione su tutti i tipi di veicoli Tesla, con e senza supervisione, in base al grado di competenza
* Garantire la massima qualitá: effettuerai riparazioni in maniera efficace e risolutiva,al primo intervento, ti occuperai dell’inspezione accurata dei veicoli da consegnare e riconsegnare ai clienti, e lavorerai con i piú alti standard di qualitá
* Collaborare: lavorando con team locali ed internazionali, offrirai e garantirai supporto, condividendo buone prassi, per poter migliorare continuamente l’esperienza di assistenza
* Prenderai in carico la tua crescita professionale: il nostro team di Formazione interno ti aiuterá a prepararti ad avere successo, fornendoti formazione riguardo alle tecnologie Tesla ed alla riparazione di Veicoli Elettrici
 **What You'll Bring*** Esperienza in ambito tecnico: esperienza professionale nella riparazione veicoli, manutenzione o assistenza tecnica in ambito automotive. aereonautico, navale, industriale, meccanico o similare
* Formazione tecnica: hai formazione tecnica specifica e abilitazione alla riparazione di veicoli in Italia
* Buone capacitá comunicative: sai parlare e scrivere in Italiano fluentemente ed hai un livello di base o intermedio di Inglese. Riesci ad esprimere il tuo parere in maniera chiara e rispettosa ed hai buone doti di ascolto
* Guida sicura: hai una patente di guida Europea (categoría B) e ti impegni a mantere uno storico di guida impeccabile
* Flessibilitá: sei disponible a lavorare su turni, che possono includere i fine settimana, per dare supporto alla tua squadra. Laddove necessario, sei disponibile a viaggiare per formazione o interventi fuori sede (Servizio Mobile o assistenza ad altri siti Tesla) ed a preparare le vetture da consegnare
* Idoneitá: sei legalmente autorizzato/a a lavorare in Italia


Tesla è un datore di lavoro che promuove le pari opportunità. Tutti i candidati qualificati verranno presi in considerazione per l'assunzione, indipendentemente da qualsiasi fattore, come stato di veterano e di disabilità, protetto da leggi locali, statali o federali applicabili.


Tesla si impegna inoltre a lavorare con persone disabili e a fornire loro sistemazioni adeguate. Comunica al selezionatore Tesla se hai bisogno di una sistemazione in qualsiasi fase del processo di colloquio.


Per un accesso rapido alla tecnologia di lettura dello schermo compatibile con questo sito, fai clic qui per scaricare un'utilità per la lettura dello schermo compatibile gratuita (un tutorial passo passo gratuito è disponibile qui). Contatta ADA@tesla.com per domande relative ad ADA o per richiedere una sistemazione ADA.


La privacy è una priorità assoluta per Tesla. È parte integrante dei nostri prodotti e la consideriamo fondamentale per la nostra attività. Per sapere di più sui dati che raccogliamo ed elaboriamo nell'ambito della tua domanda, consulta la nostra Informativa sulla privacy di Tesla per i talenti .

  

Tesla is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action employer committed to diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, gender identity or any other factor protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.  

  

Tesla is also committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. Please let your recruiter know if you need an accommodation at any point during the interview process.
company_description: Tesla is accelerating the world’s transition to sustainable energy.

title: Account Commerciale B2B - Money Aziende
description:
Money Aziende è la piattaforma di Money.it dedicata alle imprese italiane: dati di bilancio, ricerche profilate e monitoring di società clienti e competitor. Money.it è uno dei principali punti di riferimento nell'informazione economica in Italia, con milioni di lettori ogni mese.
  
Il team commerciale di Money Aziende è in costruzione — e stiamo cercando la persona giusta per crescere insieme.
  
Il ruolo
  
Collaborerai con noi come Agente Commerciale B2B in regime di collaborazione autonoma, con il compito di convertire i lead che arrivano sulla piattaforma e di sviluppare attivamente nuovi prospect nel mercato delle imprese italiane.
  
  

**Nel concreto ti occuperai di:**  

* Qualificare e lavorare i lead inbound tramite email, telefono e webcall
* Gestire le trattative end\-to\-end, dalla prima chiamata alla chiusura
* Fare prospecting attivo su LinkedIn, database e referral per costruire la tua pipeline
* Seguire i clienti acquisiti per massimizzare retention e opportunità di upsell
* Collaborare con il team di prodotto e marketing per affinare il posizionamento commerciale


Cosa cerchiamo
  
* 2\+ anni di esperienza in un ruolo commerciale B2B (media digitali, SaaS, servizi finanziari o affini)
* Ottima gestione delle trattative telefoniche e in webcall
* Approccio consulenziale: sai ascoltare prima di vendere
* Familiarità con CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce o simili)
* Proattività nel prospecting e nell'outreach
* Orientamento al risultato e ai numeri


**Sarà un plus:** esperienza nella vendita di prodotti in abbonamento o nella comunicazione finanziaria B2B.
  
Cosa offriamo
  
* Compenso strutturato: fisso mensile \+ provvigioni sulle vendite generate
* Lead qualificati già in entrata — non parti da zero
* Stack commerciale operativo (CRM, strumenti di outreach)
* Contesto in crescita con ampio spazio di autonomia e flessibilità organizzativa
* Possibilità di evoluzione del rapporto nel tempo, in funzione dei risultati


Il presente annuncio è rivolto a candidati di entrambi i sessi (D.Lgs. 198/2006\).

title: Pharma Medical Affairs
description:
Alma Laboris ha aperto le iscrizioni per i percorsi di eccellenza nell'Industria Farmaceutica, offrendo agevolazioni di inserimento professionale per il progresso di carriera. L'obiettivo primario è formare e specializzare individui da presentare alle aziende partner del network.

I requisiti per partecipare includono coloro che attualmente ricoprono ruoli aziendali, professionisti specializzati, titolari e/o collaboratori di aziende, laureati del settore e giovani professionisti provenienti da altri percorsi di studio previa valutazione. **Con la presentazione della candidatura, i partecipanti riceveranno informazioni dettagliate.**


**Il presente annuncio è rivolto a entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03\.**

title: Tecnico Senior
RESPONSABILE UFFICIO GARE
Finance, Administration & Governance Officer (m/f/d)
description:
### **Company Description**

**About EIT RawMaterials**


As the world moves towards a greener future, the demand for raw materials—minerals and metals—is set to increase immensely over the next three decades. Europe faces a critical challenge in securing a stable supply of these essential resources. This challenge is where EIT RawMaterials steps in.


EIT RawMaterials is at the forefront of driving innovation and sustainability in the raw materials sector. As an Innovation Community within the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), we collaborate with over 300 partners across industry, academia, and research institutes. Together, we forge dynamic, long\-term partnerships to develop innovative solutions that meet Europe's growing demand for raw materials.

**About the Role**
------------------


The **Senior Finance, Administration \& Governance Officer (m/f/d)** for CLC South is a key operational role responsible for ensuring the efficient management of financial, administrative, legal, and governance activities of EIT RawMaterials in Italy.


Acting as the primary local coordination point, the role serves as a bridge between the Italian entity and EIT RawMaterials Headquarters, ensuring that local requirements, regulations, and operational needs are effectively aligned with the organisation's policies, procedures, and strategic objectives.


We are looking for a highly reliable, hands\-on professional who enjoys taking ownership, solving problems proactively, and ensuring that critical operational processes run smoothly. The successful candidate will be comfortable working independently while collaborating closely with colleagues across Europe and external stakeholders in Italy.


This position is particularly suited for someone who combines strong financial and administrative expertise with excellent coordination skills, attention to detail, and a pragmatic approach to getting things done.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Serve as the primary operational contact for financial, administrative, legal, and governance matters related to the Italian entity.
* Coordinate and align local Italian requirements with EIT RawMaterials’ corporate policies, procedures, and reporting obligations.
* Manage and maintain financial and administrative documentation, ensuring accurate filing and record\-keeping.
* Support monthly financial operations, including invoice processing, bank statement management, and payroll coordination.
* Oversee expense tracking, travel reimbursements, and related financial administration.
* Monitor cash flow requirements and support financial planning activities in coordination with Headquarters.
* Prepare regular financial reports and contribute to quarterly and annual reporting processes.
* Support the preparation of annual financial statements and assist in audit processes.
* Act as the key point of contact for external accountants, auditors, payroll providers, legal advisors, banks, and public authorities.
* Support year\-end closing activities and ensure compliance with applicable Italian regulations and internal governance requirements.
* Provide administrative support related to legal and governance matters, including documentation, reporting, and compliance activities.
* Assist in the organisation of corporate governance activities, including stakeholder meetings and statutory reporting requirements.
* Collaborate closely with Headquarters and cross\-functional teams to ensure transparency, consistency, and timely execution of activities.
* Identify operational risks and proactively address issues before they become critical.
* Support activities related to EU\-funded projects and public funding programmes where relevant.
* Handle ad hoc financial, administrative, and governance\-related tasks as required.

**Key Requirements**
--------------------

* Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics, or relevant education.
* Proven experience in finance, administration, accounting, or a comparable operational role.
* Strong understanding of Italian financial and administrative requirements.
* Solid knowledge of financial processes, reporting, and compliance.
* Demonstrated ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders and manage priorities independently.
* Highly reliable, structured, and detail\-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
* Hands\-on attitude with a proactive, solution\-oriented mindset.
* Excellent organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Experience working with external stakeholders such as accountants, auditors, legal advisors, or public authorities.
* Good proficiency in MS Office, particularly Excel.
* Fluency in both Italian and English is mandatory.
* Experience working in an international organisation or European environment is highly desirable.
* Experience with EU\-funded projects, European funding programmes, or grant management is considered a strong advantage.
* Availability to join within a short timeframe is highly appreciated.

**What We Offer**
-----------------

* An opportunity to contribute to Europe's green and digital transitions by supporting one of the leading innovation ecosystems in the raw materials sector.
* A diverse, international, and collaborative working environment, with colleagues and partners from across Europe.
* A dynamic role combining finance, administration, governance, and stakeholder coordination within a European context.
* Flexible hybrid working arrangements.
* Competitive compensation aligned with experience and qualifications.

#### **Additional Information \& Recruitment Process**

#### **Commitment to Diversity**


EIT RawMaterials is committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals, regardless of background.

#### **Employment Type**


Fix\-term contract with the possibility of extension.

#### **Location**


Rome, Italy (Hybrid)


The position will remain open until a suitable candidate has been identified.

#### **Recruitment Process**

**Application Review**


Our team carefully reviews every application. Please submit your CV in English.

**First Interview (30 minutes)**


Meeting with the Hiring Manager and Recruitment Team.

**Second Interview (60 minutes)**


Interview including a presentation on a relevant topic with the Hiring Manager and HR Team.

**Offer**


Successful candidates will receive an attractive offer to join EIT RawMaterials.

**Roma, Italia (modalità ibrida)**

**Chi siamo**
-------------

### **EIT RawMaterials**


Con la transizione verso un'economia più sostenibile, la domanda di materie prime critiche, minerali e metalli è destinata ad aumentare significativamente nei prossimi decenni. Garantire un approvvigionamento sicuro e sostenibile di queste risorse rappresenta una delle principali sfide strategiche per l'Europa.


EIT RawMaterials svolge un ruolo centrale nel promuovere innovazione, competitività e sostenibilità nel settore delle materie prime. In qualità di Knowledge and Innovation Community (KIC) dell'European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), collaboriamo con oltre 300 partner tra imprese, università e centri di ricerca in tutta Europa.


Attraverso partnership di lungo termine, sosteniamo lo sviluppo di soluzioni innovative che contribuiscono a rafforzare le catene del valore europee delle materie prime e a supportare le transizioni verde e digitale.

**Il ruolo**
------------


Il/La Senior Finance, Administration \& Governance Officer per CLC South ricoprirà un ruolo operativo chiave, responsabile della gestione efficiente delle attività finanziarie, amministrative, legali e di governance di EIT RawMaterials in Italia.


La figura agirà come principale punto di coordinamento locale tra l'entità italiana e l'Headquarters di EIT RawMaterials, garantendo l'allineamento tra requisiti normativi e operativi italiani e le politiche, procedure e priorità strategiche dell'organizzazione.


Siamo alla ricerca di una persona affidabile, concreta e orientata ai risultati, capace di assumersi responsabilità, risolvere problematiche in modo proattivo e garantire il corretto funzionamento dei processi operativi essenziali.


Il ruolo è particolarmente adatto a professionisti che combinano solide competenze amministrative e finanziarie con capacità organizzative, attenzione al dettaglio e un approccio pragmatico alla gestione delle attività.

**Principali responsabilità**
-----------------------------

* Essere il principale punto di riferimento operativo per questioni finanziarie, amministrative, legali e di governance relative all'entità italiana.
* Garantire l'allineamento dei requisiti normativi e operativi italiani con le politiche, procedure e obblighi di reporting di EIT RawMaterials.
* Gestire e mantenere la documentazione amministrativa e finanziaria assicurandone accuratezza e corretta archiviazione.
* Supportare le attività finanziarie periodiche, inclusa la gestione delle fatture, dei movimenti bancari e il coordinamento delle attività payroll.
* Monitorare note spese, rimborsi viaggio e relativa amministrazione finanziaria.
* Supportare il monitoraggio dei fabbisogni di cassa e le attività di pianificazione finanziaria in coordinamento con l'Headquarters.
* Preparare report finanziari periodici e contribuire ai processi di reporting trimestrali e annuali.
* Supportare la predisposizione del bilancio annuale e le attività di revisione contabile.
* Coordinare i rapporti con commercialisti, revisori, consulenti del lavoro, consulenti legali, istituti bancari e autorità pubbliche.
* Supportare le attività di chiusura annuale e garantire il rispetto delle normative italiane applicabili e dei requisiti interni di governance.
* Fornire supporto amministrativo per attività legali e di governance, inclusa la predisposizione della documentazione e degli adempimenti richiesti.
* Collaborare all'organizzazione delle attività societarie e di governance, incluse assemblee dei soci e obblighi statutari.
* Collaborare con l'Headquarters e con team internazionali per garantire trasparenza, coerenza e tempestiva esecuzione delle attività.
* Identificare rischi operativi e proporre soluzioni preventive.
* Supportare attività collegate a progetti finanziati dall'Unione Europea e programmi di finanziamento pubblici, ove applicabile.
* Gestire ulteriori attività amministrative, finanziarie e di governance secondo necessità.

**Requisiti**
-------------

* Laurea in Economia, Finanza, Amministrazione Aziendale o discipline equivalenti.
* Esperienza professionale in ambito amministrativo, finanziario, contabile o in ruoli operativi analoghi.
* Buona conoscenza degli aspetti amministrativi, fiscali e finanziari applicabili in Italia.
* Solida conoscenza dei processi finanziari, di reporting e compliance.
* Capacità dimostrata di coordinare molteplici interlocutori e gestire priorità in autonomia.
* Elevato livello di affidabilità, precisione e senso di responsabilità.
* Approccio pratico, proattivo e orientato alla risoluzione dei problemi.
* Ottime capacità organizzative, comunicative e relazionali.
* Esperienza nella gestione di rapporti con commercialisti, revisori, consulenti legali, enti pubblici o altri stakeholder esterni.
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office, in particolare Excel.
* Ottima conoscenza della lingua italiana e inglese.
* Costituisce titolo preferenziale l'esperienza maturata in contesti internazionali o europei.
* Costituisce un forte vantaggio l'esperienza con progetti finanziati dall'UE, programmi europei o gestione di grant.
* Disponibilità ad iniziare in tempi brevi sarà considerata un elemento preferenziale.

**Cosa offriamo**
-----------------

* L'opportunità di contribuire alle transizioni verde e digitale dell'Europa all'interno di uno dei principali ecosistemi europei dell'innovazione nel settore delle materie prime.
* Un ambiente di lavoro internazionale, inclusivo e collaborativo, con colleghi e partner provenienti da tutta Europa.
* Un ruolo dinamico che combina attività finanziarie, amministrative, di governance e coordinamento degli stakeholder in un contesto europeo.
* Modalità di lavoro flessibile in formula ibrida.
* Pacchetto retributivo competitivo e commisurato all'esperienza e alle competenze.

**Informazioni aggiuntive e processo di selezione**
---------------------------------------------------

#### **Diversità e inclusione**


EIT RawMaterials promuove una cultura inclusiva e valorizza la diversità. Invitiamo a candidarsi tutte le persone qualificate, indipendentemente dal background personale o professionale.

#### **Tipologia contrattuale**


Contratto di lavoro a tempo determinato con possibilità di rinnovo.

#### **Sede di lavoro**


Roma (modalità ibrida).


La posizione rimarrà aperta fino all'individuazione del candidato o della candidata idonea.

#### **Processo di selezione**

**Valutazione delle candidature**


Tutte le candidature saranno attentamente esaminate. Si richiede l'invio del CV in lingua inglese.

**Primo colloquio (30 minuti)**


Colloquio con il Hiring Manager e il team Recruitment.

**Secondo colloquio (60 minuti)**


Colloquio con presentazione su un tema rilevante, alla presenza del Hiring Manager e del team HR.

**Offerta**


I candidati selezionati riceveranno una proposta economica competitiva per entrare a far parte di EIT RawMaterials.
